science: Honey can remain edible for thousands of years when sealed from moisture.
The human brain uses roughly one fifth of the body's total energy.
A teaspoon of neutron-star matter would weigh millions of tons on Earth.
Water can boil and freeze simultaneously at its triple point.
Human DNA in one body would stretch billions of kilometers if uncoiled.
Glass is an amorphous solid, not a true liquid.
Lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun.
Your body replaces a large fraction of its cells over time.
space: Sunlight takes about 8 minutes to travel from the Sun to Earth.
A day on Venus is longer than a Venus year.
The International Space Station orbits Earth about every 90 minutes.
One million Earths could fit inside the Sun.
Saturn's rings are wide but surprisingly thin compared to their diameter.
The Milky Way and Andromeda are expected to merge in several billion years.
Space is silent because sound needs a medium.
Mars hosts Olympus Mons, the tallest known volcano in the solar system.
animals: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they do not drift apart.
A group of owls is called a parliament.
Crows can recognize human faces for years.
Dolphins use unique signature whistles, similar to names.
Goats have rectangular pupils that improve panoramic vision.
Mantis shrimps strike so fast they create cavitation bubbles.
history: Cleopatra lived closer to the Moon landing than to the Great Pyramid's construction.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
Nintendo began in 1889 as a playing-card company.
The shortest recorded war lasted less than one hour.
The Great Fire of London destroyed thousands of homes in 1666.
The Apollo missions returned moon rocks that are still studied today.
The first modern Olympics were held in 1896 in Athens.
The printing press transformed literacy and information distribution in Europe.
math: Shuffling a deck usually creates an ordering never seen before.
In a group of 23 people, shared birthdays are more likely than most expect.
A Mobius strip has one side and one boundary edge.
Euler's identity links five fundamental constants in one equation.
Prime numbers are the building blocks of whole numbers.
Pi is irrational, so its decimal expansion never repeats exactly.
The Monty Hall problem rewards switching doors.
The Collatz conjecture is easy to state but still unproven.
technology: The first computer bug was literally a moth found in hardware.
Email predates the World Wide Web by decades.
The first 1 GB hard drive weighed hundreds of pounds.
GPS works only because satellite clocks account for relativity.
Open-source software powers much of the modern internet stack.
Early mobile phones had less computing power than simple modern watches.
Compression formats make streaming media practical at global scale.
The Apollo Guidance Computer had tiny memory by today's standards.
earth: Earth's tectonic plates move about as fast as fingernails grow.
More fresh water is locked in ice than in rivers and lakes combined.
The Amazon helps generate much of its own rainfall.
Ocean currents transport massive amounts of heat around the planet.
Coral reefs support a high share of marine biodiversity.
The deepest ocean trench is deeper than Everest is tall.
Volcanic eruptions can cool climate temporarily by reflecting sunlight.
Mangroves protect coastlines from erosion and storm damage.
